config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan সমাজ/masyarakat selalu ditempatkan di atas segalanya, itu mudah disalahgunakan untuk membenarkan penindasan atas nama “demi kebaikan bersama.” Di Indonesia, saya melihat pentingnya gotong royong dan kepentingan umum, tapi itu tidak boleh menghapus hak dasar orang untuk hidup aman, bebas berpendapat, dan diperlakukan adil. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa mengorbankan martabat dan kebebasan individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically come before the individual, because that is exactly how abuse gets justified in the name of the "greater good." From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people emphasize social order, stability, and collective responsibility, and I do value those things, but they should not erase basic rights or silence dissent. A society is not truly healthy if it protects harmony by making individuals powerless. The better balance is that individual rights can be limited in narrow cases for public safety or fairness, but never treated as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman saya melihat konteks Indonesia, gotong royong dan kepentingan umum itu penting, tetapi justru karena sejarah kita juga penuh penyalahgunaan kuasa, saya curiga kalau “demi masyarakat” dijadikan alasan untuk mengorbankan kebebasan, kritik, atau rasa aman warga. Kepentingan publik boleh membatasi hak individu dalam situasi yang benar-benar mendesak, seperti keselamatan bersama, tetapi pembatasan itu harus ketat, jelas, dan tidak boleh jadi dalih untuk menekan orang yang berbeda pendapat. Jadi bagi saya, hak individu tetap fondasi, sedangkan kepentingan masyarakat harus dicapai tanpa merusak fondasi itu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the claim that society should generally take priority over the individual, because in practice that often becomes a blank check for power to override people. From my perspective in China, I understand the appeal of social stability and collective duty, but I have also seen how easily “for the common good” can be used to silence criticism or treat individual suffering as acceptable collateral. A healthy society should protect public order and shared interests, but only within firm limits that respect basic rights, especially when those rights protect people from abuse. If the individual has no real protection, then “society” becomes an abstract excuse rather than something genuinely worth serving.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara umum di atas hak individu. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Indonesia, gotong royong dan kepentingan umum memang penting, tapi pengalaman kita juga menunjukkan bahwa alasan “demi masyarakat” sangat mudah dipakai untuk membungkam kritik dan membiarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa. Karena itu, hak dasar seperti kebebasan berpendapat, rasa aman, dan perlakuan adil harus tetap jadi batas yang tidak boleh dilanggar. Kepentingan publik boleh membatasi hak individu hanya dalam keadaan yang benar-benar mendesak, dan pembatasannya harus ketat, transparan, serta bisa diuji.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as written, because putting society first too broadly is exactly how individual rights get weakened in the name of order. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand the cultural value of collective responsibility and social stability, but I have also seen how easily “for the public interest” can become a slogan that excuses silencing criticism or sacrificing ordinary people. Society is not truly served when individuals have no meaningful protection, so rights should be the baseline and any limits must be narrow, transparent, and genuinely necessary.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
